text:
Once upon a time, there was a little owl named Oliver. Oliver loved to lurk in ancient oak trees and listen to bees. One autumn day, he decided to travel south.

Oliver soared gracefully across vast fields strewn with fallen leaves. He visited fields where harvest moons painted everything gold. He slept somewhere muddy beneath an old hornbeam.

On his journey, Oliver met a brown rabbit. The rabbit had holes everywhere. "Take shelter," he whispered. Oliver did. Snow fell. Oliver dreamed of worms and apples.

After many adventures, Oliver rested beneath a twilight sky. He thought of home. Owl ears heard chatter. Wise old chickens said, "There's danger. Take sticks." Oliver hurried.

A fox sneaked. Oliver flew loud. The fox screeched. Oliver flew again. The fox chased. Oliver hid in tufts of cotton.

Then Oliver saw feathers. A snake coiled nearby. Oliver flew away clutching sticks.

No beast bothered little ol feathers thereafter.
